step_by_step_thinking:
Lateral shift (Bennett's movement) affects the mesiodistal position of the tooth because Bennett's movement involves sliding the distal segment relative to the mesial segment along the long axis of the jawbone.
\boxed{B} Based on the information given about Bennett's movement, which involves shifting one segment relative to another along the long axis of the jawbone, we understand that it primarily impacts the mesiodistal position of the teeth rather than just their faciolingual position. Therefore, option B correctly identifies this effect.
